title: OpenClaw AI 使用技巧：打造高效智能工作流
content: TL;DR — OpenClaw 是一款强大的 AI 效率工具，本文将分享 10+ 实用技巧，帮助你最大化发挥其潜力。8 min read. OpenClaw 作为新一代 AI 效率工具，正在改变我们与 AI 交互的方式。无论你是开发者、内容创作者还是知识工作者，掌握这些技巧都能让你的工作效率提升 10 倍。 🚀 快速入门什么是 OpenClaw？OpenClaw 是一款集成了多种 AI 能力的智能助手平台，支持： 多模型切换：无缝切换 GPT-4、Claude、Gemini 等主流模型 知识库管理：构建个人/团队知识库，实现 RAG 检索增强 工作流自动化：创建可复用的 AI 工作流 协作功能：团队共享 Prompt 和对话模板 安装与配置 首次启动后，前往 Settings > API Keys 配置你的 API 密钥。 💡 核心技巧1. 快捷键大师掌握快捷键是提升效率的第一步： 快捷键 功能 Cmd/Ctrl + N 新建对话 Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + K 快速搜索知识库 Cmd/Ctrl + / 切换 AI 模型 Cmd/Ctrl + Enter 发送消息 Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + S 保存为模板 Esc 清空当前输入 Pro Tip: 使用 Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P 打开命令面板，可以快速访问所有功能。 2. Prompt 模板管理创建可复用的 Prompt 模板是提高效率的关键： 使用变量: 在模板中使用 {{variable}} 语法定义变量，使用时会自动弹出输入框。 3. 知识库构建技巧OpenClaw 的知识库功能支持 RAG（检索增强生成），让你的 AI 拥有上下文记忆： 最佳实践： 分类组织：按项目/主题创建不同的知识库 定期更新：设置自动同步，保持知识库最新 添加元数据：为文档添加标签，提高检索精度 4. 多模型协作策略不同模型有不同优势，合理切换可以获得最佳效果： 任务类型 推荐模型 原因 代码生成 Claude 3.5 Sonnet 代码能力强，上下文长 创意写作 GPT-4 创意丰富，表达自然 快速问答 GPT-3.5 / Claude Haiku 速度快，成本低 复杂推理 Claude 3 Opus 推理能力强 多模态任务 GPT-4 Vision / Gemini Pro 支持图像理解 Pro Tip: 使用 OpenClaw 的「模型对比」功能，同时向多个模型发送相同请求，对比结果。 5. 工作流自动化创建自动化工作流，一键完成复杂任务： 6. 上下文管理有效管理对话上下文，避免 token 浪费： 使用 /clear 清除无关上下文 使用 /focus 聚焦当前话题 使用 /summarize 压缩历史对话 7. 批量处理技巧OpenClaw 支持批量处理，适合处理大量相似任务： 8. 团队协作功能OpenClaw 的团队功能让知识共享更简单： 共享 Prompt 库：团队成员可以共享和使用经过验证的 Prompt 对话分享：一键分享有价值的对话记录 知识库权限：设置不同级别的访问权限 9. API 集成将 OpenClaw 集成到你的工作流中： 10. 调试与优化当 AI 回答不理想时，使用调试功能： 查看检索上下文：了解 AI 参考了哪些文档 Token 分析：分析 token 使用情况 温度调节：调整创造性 vs 准确性 🎯 场景实战场景一：代码审查助手 场景二：技术文档写作 场景三：学习助手 ⚡ 高级技巧自定义命令在 ~/.openclaw/commands.yaml 中定义自定义命令： 使用方式：输入 /review 即可触发。 Webhook 集成设置 Webhook，将 AI 能力集成到其他系统： 插件开发OpenClaw 支持插件扩展： 📊 性能优化Token 优化策略 精简 Prompt：删除不必要的描述 使用引用：用 ID 引用而非重复内容 分批处理：大任务拆分为小任务 缓存结果：相似请求复用结果 成本控制 🔮 最佳实践总结 模板先行：为常见任务创建模板 知识库为王：持续维护和更新知识库 模型匹配：根据任务选择合适的模型 迭代优化：不断改进 Prompt 和工作流 团队协作：共享最佳实践和资源 📚 资源链接 OpenClaw 官方文档 Prompt 工程指南 社区模板库 💡 提示: 本文基于 OpenClaw v2.0 版本编写，部分功能可能在新版本中有所变化。 你有使用 OpenClaw 的独特技巧吗？欢迎在评论区分享！

title: AI Agents Rising: The Most Important AI Trend of 2025
content: TL;DR — LLMs answer questions. Agents do things. This is the paradigm shift of 2025. 12 min read. If 2023 was the year of the LLM, 2025 is the year of the Agent. The difference? An agent doesn't just chat — it plans, executes, iterates, and delivers. Let's dive into the technology that's reshaping how we work with AI. 🧠 Core Concept: What's an Agent?LLM vs Agent Key difference: Agents have autonomy and agency. They don't just talk — they act. 🏗️ Four Pillars of Agency Pillar Description Analogy 🧭 Planning Decompose goals into executable steps Travel planning → book flight → reserve hotel 💾 Memory Retain context, history, learned patterns Remember your preferences 🔧 Tool Use Call search, code exec, APIs, etc. Search web, run Python, query DB 🔄 Reflection Evaluate results, self-correct "That failed, let me try another approach" 2. Major Agent Frameworks📊 Framework Landscape Framework Language Superpower Best For LangChain Python/JS Richest ecosystem Rapid prototyping LangGraph Python State machine workflows Complex pipelines CrewAI Python Multi-agent teams Collaborative tasks AutoGPT Python Autonomous execution Experimental projects Assistants API REST Production-ready Enterprise use 🔷 LangChain / LangGraphThe most popular agent framework. Massive ecosystem. LangGraph State Machine: 👥 CrewAI — Multi-Agent TeamsMultiple agents collaborating on complex tasks. Collaboration Flow: 🟢 OpenAI Assistants APIProduction-grade official solution from OpenAI. 3. Real-World Applications💻 Scenario 1: Coding AgentClaude Code and Cursor Composer exemplify coding agents: 📚 Scenario 2: Research AgentAutonomous research and synthesis: ⚙️ Scenario 3: Automation AgentConnect tools and services: 📊 Scenario 4: Data Analysis AgentEnd-to-end analysis pipeline: 4. Agent Design Best Practices✅ Design Principles Principle Description Example Clear boundaries Define scope explicitly Data agent only analyzes data Single-purpose tools Each tool does one thing search_web(query) only searches Graceful failure Handle errors elegantly Max 3 retries Human-in-loop Confirm critical decisions Ask before deleting files ⚠️ Common Pitfalls Pitfall Consequence Mitigation Over-permissioned Dangerous operations Sandbox + permission scoping Infinite loops Resource exhaustion Step count limit Hallucinated actions Claims false completion Verify execution results Cost runaway Unexpected bills Budget caps 5. Challenges & Risks🚧 Technical Challenges 🔒 Security Risks Risk Description Mitigation Permission abuse Execute dangerous actions Minimal privilege principle Data leakage Auto-process sensitive info Data sanitization Opacity Can't explain decisions Comprehensive logging 6. What's Next 🔮 Agent evolution in H2 2025 Trend Description 🧠 Better reasoning o-series, Claude extended thinking make agents smarter 👁️ Multimodal agents See, hear, speak — unified interface 👥 Agent orchestration Teams of specialized agents 📱 On-device agents Privacy-first, run locally 🏥 Domain agents Medical, legal, finance specialists Agents are evolving from "can chat" to "can ship." This isn't just a capability upgrade — it's a paradigm shift in how we work with AI. Are you ready? Bottom line: AI Agents are the 2025 story. Master agent development, master the future of AI applications. Start with LangChain, go deep with LangGraph. Related: Build Your AI Workflow → RAG Complete Guide →

title: AI Image Generation 2025: Midjourney vs DALL-E 3 vs Stable Diffusion
content: TL;DR — Three tools, three philosophies. Match your needs to their strengths. 12 min read. AI image generation has graduated from novelty to production tool. Whether you're a designer, content creator, or dev, knowing which tool for which job is the edge. Let's go deep. ⚡ 3-Second Picker You need... Pick Because Maximum visual quality Midjourney Artistic rendering is unmatched Text in images DALL-E 3 Only one that reliably renders text Full control SD/FLUX Open source, infinite customization Zero budget SD/FLUX Free (just need GPU) Fastest start DALL-E 3 Built into ChatGPT 1. Midjourney — The Art King Position: Visual quality ceiling. Best artistic rendering. 🎨 The Basics 📐 Prompt Formula Example: ⚙️ Parameter Cheat Sheet Param What Example Effect --ar Aspect ratio --ar 16:9 Set dimensions --v Model version --v 6.1 Use V6.1 --s Stylization --s 250 0-1000, higher = more artistic --q Quality --q 2 1 or 2 --c Chaos --c 50 0-100, higher = more variation --no Negative prompt --no text Exclude elements 🚀 Pro TechniquesCharacter Consistency (cref): cw controls how much to borrow: 0 = face only, 100 = face + outfit + hair Style Reference (sref): Seamless Patterns: 2. DALL-E 3 — The Conversational Artist Position: Best for text rendering. Most intuitive interface. 🗣️ Conversational GenerationJust describe to ChatGPT: Unique superpower: DALL-E 3 can reliably render readable text in images. 💻 API Usage 🎯 DALL-E 3 Exclusives Feature Description Text rendering Display correct text in images — logos, headers, covers Prompt adherence Faithfully follows complex multi-element descriptions Iterative editing "Change the cat to a dog" — modify in conversation 3. Stable Diffusion / FLUX — The Freedom Fighter Position: Maximum control. Open source. 🛠️ Setup ✍️ Prompt Craft 🎛️ ControlNet Precision Control Type Use Case Input Lineart Composition control Line drawing OpenPose Human pose control Pose reference Depth Spatial depth Depth map Canny Edge control Edge detection IP-Adapter Character consistency Reference image 4. Battle Testing🖼️ Blog Cover ArtMidjourney: DALL-E 3: 📦 Product ConceptMidjourney: 🧑‍🎨 Character ConsistencyMidjourney: Stable Diffusion: IP-Adapter for character consistency + ControlNet for pose 🏷️ Logo DesignDALL-E 3 (text): 5. Prompt Engineering 201Universal Template Quality Enhancers Category Keywords Quality masterpiece, best quality, ultra-detailed, 8K Lighting volumetric, ray tracing, rim light, golden hour Photo shot on Sony A7R IV, 85mm f/1.4, bokeh Style oil painting, watercolor, concept art, cyberpunk 6. Licensing Quick Guide Tool Commercial Notes Midjourney ✅ Paid OK Read ToS carefully DALL-E 3 ✅ Full rights Clearest licensing SD/FLUX ✅ Open Check training data source Best practices: 🚫 Avoid generating real people's faces ✅ Respect artist rights 📝 Label AI-generated content Bottom line: Midjourney for quality, DALL-E 3 for text & ease, SD/FLUX for control. Mix and match. The creative edge is knowing when to use which. Related: AI Agents Rising → Build Your AI Workflow →

title: 2025 AI Coding Tools Showdown: Claude Code vs Cursor vs GitHub Copilot
content: TL;DR — Three AI coding tools enter, one workflow wins. 10 min read. The AI coding assistant space has evolved from "nice-to-have" to "can't-live-without." In 2025, three tools dominate the conversation: Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot. I've spent months using all three in production — here's my honest, in-depth comparison. ⚡ Quick Verdict You want... Pick this Because Terminal-first workflow Claude Code CLI-native, strongest agentic ability AI-native IDE Cursor Zero-config, visual multi-file editing Fluid code completion Copilot Best inline suggestions, lowest price Maximum power Combine them Each excels at different things 1. Tool Profiles🔷 Claude Code One-liner: A CLI-based AI programmer that actually ships code autonomously. Built by Anthropic on top of Claude. Not a chatbot — an agent that plans, executes, and verifies. Capability Matrix: Ability Rating Agentic Coding ★★★★★ Codebase Understanding ★★★★★ Git Integration ★★★★★ MCP Extensibility ★★★★★ Multi-file Refactor ★★★★☆ Best for: Terminal power users, complex refactors, agentic workflows 🔶 Cursor One-liner: VS Code reimagined from the ground up for AI. Forked from VS Code with AI baked into every surface. The zero-config experience is unmatched. Capability Matrix: Ability Rating Composer (Multi-file) ★★★★★ Multi-model Support ★★★★★ Out-of-box Experience ★★★★★ @codebase Search ★★★★☆ Visual Diff Preview ★★★★☆ Best for: Frontend/full-stack devs, VS Code loyalists, instant productivity 🔴 GitHub Copilot One-liner: The king of inline completion, with the widest IDE support. Microsoft/GitHub's AI pair programmer. The inline suggestion experience remains the gold standard. Capability Matrix: Ability Rating Inline Completion ★★★★★ IDE Ecosystem ★★★★★ Price-to-value ★★★★★ Copilot Chat ★★★★☆ Workspace Flow ★★★★☆ Best for: Daily coding, JetBrains users, budget-conscious teams 2. Head-to-Head Comparison Dimension Claude Code Cursor Copilot Interaction CLI conversation IDE chat + edit Inline + Chat Multi-file Edit ★★★★★ ★★★★★ ★★★☆☆ Codebase Understanding ★★★★★ ★★★★★ ★★★☆☆ Model Choice Claude only Multi-model GPT only Terminal ★★★★★ Native ★★★★☆ Built-in ★★★☆☆ Needs config Git Integration ★★★★★ Deep ★★☆☆☆ None ★★★★☆ Workspace Context Window 200K tokens Model-dependent Limited MCP Support ✅ ❌ ❌ Learning Curve 📈 Medium 📉 Low 📉 Low Personal Price $20/mo $20/mo $10/mo ✅ 3. Real-World Scenarios🏗️ Scenario 1: Greenfield ProjectWinner: Claude Code Claude Code autonomously: ✅ Scaffolds the project structure ✅ Installs all dependencies ✅ Configures Tailwind + MDX ✅ Runs initial tests ✅ Sets up deployment config Cursor Composer is equally strong here, with better visual preview. Copilot is weaker for greenfield — it shines in existing codebases. 🐛 Scenario 2: Debug & FixWinner: Claude Code Full closed-loop debugging: Key difference: Copilot Chat can analyze errors but can't run code. Cursor can read terminal output but needs manual trigger. 🔧 Scenario 3: Large-Scale RefactorTie: Claude Code & Cursor Tool Edge Claude Code Precise step-by-step control with human-in-the-loop Cursor Visual diff makes reviewing changes intuitive ⌨️ Scenario 4: Daily Coding FlowWinner: GitHub Copilot The inline completion experience is still unmatched: ⚡ Sub-100ms latency 🎯 Context-aware suggestions 🌊 Zero interruption to flow state Why it matters: 80% of daily coding is routine completion. Copilot dominates this surface area. 4. Pricing Breakdown Tier Claude Code Cursor Copilot Free Rate-limited 2000 completions/mo Students/oss free Individual $20/mo $20/mo $10/mo ✅ Team Usage-based $40/mo $19/mo Enterprise Custom Custom $39/mo Value analysis: 🥇 Cheapest entry: Copilot ($10/mo) 🥈 Most features: Cursor ($20/mo, multi-model) 🥉 Most powerful: Claude Code (unmatched agentic ability) 5. Decision Guide🎯 Pick Claude Code if you: ✅ Live in the terminal ✅ Need AI to autonomously complete complex tasks ✅ Want deep codebase understanding ✅ Need MCP tool integrations ✅ Care about Git-native workflows 🎯 Pick Cursor if you: ✅ Are a VS Code power user ✅ Want zero-config AI out of the box ✅ Need to switch between AI models freely ✅ Prefer visual editing + preview ✅ Work primarily frontend/full-stack 🎯 Pick Copilot if you: ✅ Mainly need inline code completion ✅ Use JetBrains IDEs ✅ Need strong team collaboration features ✅ Are budget-sensitive ($10/mo) ✅ Want the most mature ecosystem 6. Pro Move: Stack Them The real power move isn't choosing one — it's combining them. These tools aren't competitors — they're complementary forces. The most productive devs I know use 2-3 of them in tandem. Bottom line: In 2025, the question isn't "which AI tool is best?" — it's "which combination fits my workflow?" Start with free tiers, experiment, and optimize. Related: Mainstream LLM Deep Dive → Build Your AI Workflow →

title: RAG Complete Guide: Give Your LLM Private Knowledge
content: TL;DR — LLMs are powerful but blind to your private data. RAG is the bridge. This guide covers everything from first principles to production. 15 min read. LLMs are incredible — until you ask about your own data. RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) fixes this by letting models look things up before answering. No fine-tuning required. Here's the complete playbook. 🧠 Why RAG?The LLM Blind Spots Limitation Impact Severity 📅 Knowledge cutoff Can't answer about recent events 🔴 Critical 🔒 Private data blind Zero access to internal docs 🔴 Critical 🎭 Hallucination risk May fabricate plausible-sounding answers 🟡 High Solution Comparison Approach How Pros Cons Rating Fine-tuning Continue training on domain data Deep adaptation Expensive, slow to update ★★★☆☆ RAG Retrieve docs → feed to model Real-time, explainable Needs retrieval infra ★★★★★ Long Context Stuff everything into context Simple Expensive, has limits ★★★★☆ RAG is the industry standard — best balance of quality, cost, and maintainability. 1. How RAG Works🔄 The Pipeline 🔑 Key ConceptsEmbeddingConvert text → vector. Semantically similar text → nearby vectors. Vector Databases Database Type Strength Best For Pinecone Managed Zero ops Production Milvus Open source High performance Large scale Chroma Open source Easy to start Prototyping Qdrant Open source Feature-rich Flexible use pgvector Extension PG-native Existing PG setups 2. Build a RAG SystemStep 1: Document Processing 💡 Chunking is critical Too large → imprecise retrieval Too small → incomplete context Sweet spot: 500-1000 chars with overlap Step 2: Embed & Store Step 3: Retrieve & Generate 3. Advanced Optimizations🚀 Query RewritingUser queries are often vague. Rewrite for clarity: 🚀 Hybrid SearchCombine vector + keyword search for best of both worlds: Method Strength Weakness Vector only Semantic understanding Weak exact match Keyword only Exact match No semantic understanding Hybrid Both More complex 🚀 Cross-Encoder RerankingFine-grained relevance scoring: 🚀 Metadata FilteringPrecision filtering on document attributes: 4. Framework Comparison Framework Focus Strength Best For LangChain General Ecosystem Quick prototypes LlamaIndex RAG-native Purpose-built Knowledge apps Haystack Production Enterprise-ready Production deploy 🔷 LangChain Quick Start 🔶 LlamaIndex Quick Start 5. Production Use Cases🏢 Enterprise Knowledge Base 💻 Code Assistant 🎧 AI Customer Support 6. Troubleshooting Guide❓ Irrelevant Retrieval Root Cause Fix Bad chunking Tune chunk_size / overlap Weak embeddings Upgrade embedding model Vector-only search Add hybrid search Poor ranking Add cross-encoder reranking ❓ Hallucinated AnswersFix: Ground the model explicitly: ❓ Slow Response Bottleneck Optimization Slow retrieval Reduce k, use faster vector DB Slow embedding Cache embeddings, use smaller model Slow generation Use faster LLM, quantized models Overall latency Cache frequent queries 7. Best Practices Rule Why Garbage in → Garbage out Document quality > algorithm optimization Chunk wisely Different doc types need different strategies Retrieve precisely Fewer, better results > many, noisy ones Cite sources Let users verify answers Iterate forever Collect feedback, improve continuously RAG is the key technology that makes AI actually useful for your private data. Master it, and you unlock a world of production AI applications. Bottom line: RAG = Retrieve + Generate. Let the model "look it up" before answering. Start with LangChain, go deep with custom pipelines. Related: AI Agents Rising → Build Your AI Workflow →

title: LLM Landscape 2025: The Complete Guide to GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama & Beyond
content: TL;DR — The 2025 LLM market is a multi-polar world. No single model wins everywhere. Here's how to pick the right one for your use case. 15 min read. The LLM landscape in 2025 is radically different from a year ago. OpenAI no longer dominates alone — Claude, Gemini, Llama, and DeepSeek are all legitimate contenders. This guide cuts through the noise with benchmarks, pricing, and real-world recommendations. 🗺️ The Map 1. OpenAI GPT Series Position: The incumbent. Richest ecosystem, widest API adoption. 📋 Model Lineup Model Released Context Role Input Price Output Price GPT-4.5 2025.02 128K Flagship chat $2.50 $10.00 GPT-4o 2024.05 128K Multimodal flagship $2.50 $10.00 o3 2025.01 200K Reasoning king — — o3-mini 2025.01 200K Lightweight reasoner $1.10 $4.40 GPT-4o mini 2024.07 128K Budget champion $0.15 $0.60 ⭐ Capability Profile Dimension Rating Notes Overall ★★★★★ Still the most balanced Coding ★★★★☆ Strong, but Claude caught up Reasoning ★★★★★ o-series is the benchmark Multimodal ★★★★★ Voice + Vision + Image gen Chinese ★★★★☆ Good, not great Ecosystem ★★★★★ Most mature API, most plugins ✅ / ❌ ✅ Most mature API ecosystem ✅ o-series reasoning models are unmatched for math/logic ✅ Full multimodal (text + image + audio generation) ❌ Premium pricing ❌ Closed source ❌ Reasoning models are slow 2. Anthropic Claude Series Position: The challenger. Best-in-class coding and safety. 📋 Model Lineup Model Released Context Role Input Price Output Price Claude Opus 4 2025.04 200K Top-tier reasoning $15.00 $75.00 Claude Sonnet 4 2025.04 200K High-performance all-rounder $3.00 $15.00 Claude 3.5 Haiku 2024.11 200K Fast & cheap $0.80 $4.00 ⭐ Capability Profile Dimension Rating Notes Overall ★★★★★ First tier Coding ★★★★★ Current #1 Reasoning ★★★★★ Opus 4 matches o3 Long Context ★★★★☆ 200K, solid Safety ★★★★★ Industry gold standard Ecosystem ★★★★☆ Growing fast ✅ / ❌ ✅ Best code generation & debugging ✅ 200K context window ✅ Safety & interpretability lead ✅ MCP protocol for extensibility ❌ No image generation ❌ Smaller API ecosystem than OpenAI ❌ Opus 4 is expensive 3. Google Gemini Series Position: The long-context king. Deep Google ecosystem integration. 📋 Model Lineup Model Released Context Role Input Price Output Price Gemini 2.5 Pro 2025.03 1M Reasoning flagship $1.25 $10.00 Gemini 2.0 Flash 2024.12 1M Fast & efficient $0.10 $0.40 Gemini 1.5 Pro 2024.02 2M Ultra-long context $1.25 $5.00 ✅ / ❌ ✅ Million-token context — unmatched for long docs ✅ Google ecosystem integration (Docs, Drive, etc.) ✅ Flash is absurdly cheap ✅ Native multimodal architecture ❌ Creativity sometimes lacking ❌ API docs UX needs work ❌ Chinese capability below domestic models 4. Meta Llama Series (Open Source) Position: The open-source champion. Deploy anywhere, customize freely. 📋 Model Lineup Model Released Context Highlight Llama 4 Maverick 2025.04 1M Multimodal, open-source strongest Llama 4 Scout 2025.04 10M Open-source longest context Llama 3.1 405B 2024.07 128K Previous-gen flagship ✅ / ❌ ✅ Open source — self-host, fine-tune, own your data ✅ Vibrant community, rich fine-tuning ecosystem ✅ Llama 4 is a massive capability leap ❌ Requires infra expertise to deploy ❌ Still trails closed-source flagships ❌ GPU costs for self-hosting 5. Other Key Models Model Maker Killer Feature Best For 🔴 DeepSeek-R1 DeepSeek Open-source reasoning, insane value Budget reasoning 🔴 DeepSeek-V3 DeepSeek Open-source general, great Chinese Chinese content 🟡 Qwen 2.5 Alibaba Best Chinese performance Chinese-first apps ⚪ Mistral Large Mistral European, efficient EU compliance 🟤 Grok xAI Real-time info, witty style Real-time news 🟢 GLM-4 Zhipu Strong bilingual Bilingual scenarios 6. Leaderboards🏆 Coding 🏆 Math & Reasoning 🏆 Long Context 🏆 Chinese 7. Use Case → Model Picker Use Case Primary Backup Why Casual chat GPT-4o mini Haiku Cheap & fast Coding assistant Sonnet 4 GPT-4o Best code model Hard reasoning o3 Opus 4 Deepest reasoning Long doc analysis Gemini 2.5 Pro Llama 4 Scout Longest context Self-hosting Llama 4 DeepSeek Open source Chinese content Qwen 2.5 DeepSeek-V3 Best Chinese Budget-conscious Gemini Flash GPT-4o mini Cheapest per token 8. What's Next (H2 2025) 🔮 Five predictions 🧠 Reasoning goes mainstream — o-series & extended thinking make complex tasks reliable 👁️ Multimodal unification — One model, all modalities (text/image/audio/video) 🤖 Agent era — From Q&A to autonomous task execution 🔓 Open source catches up — Llama 4 & DeepSeek are closing the gap fast 📱 On-device LLMs — Small models run locally on phones/laptops The era of "one model to rule them all" is over. Pick the right model for the right job — that's the 2025 way. Bottom line: No single model wins everywhere. Build your stack around your use cases, not brand loyalty. Related: AI Coding Tools Showdown → Build Your AI Workflow →

title: Build Your AI Workflow: The Practical Playbook for 10x Engineers
content: TL;DR — Don't chase the "best tool." Build the right combination. Here's my battle-tested AI workflow stack. 10 min read. AI tools are powerful individually, but compose them right and you multiply the effect. The problem? Most people have too many tools and no system. This post shares what actually works after months of production use. 💡 Core Philosophy Don't optimize for the strongest tool. Optimize for the strongest workflow. Design Principles 1. My AI Stack💻 Coding Task Primary Backup Why Daily coding Copilot — Inline completion, zero friction Complex refactor Claude Code Cursor Full repo context, autonomous execution Debug Claude Code — Can run tests, closed-loop fix Code review Sonnet 4 GPT-4o Understands context, precise suggestions Rapid prototype Cursor Claude Code Visual, multi-file Composer ✍️ Writing Task Primary Backup Why Ideation Opus 4 GPT-4o Creative & logical Drafting Sonnet 4 GPT-4o Fast, high quality Editing GPT-4o Claude Flexible style tuning Translation DeepSeek GPT-4o Accurate & natural 🔍 Research Task Primary Backup Why Web search Perplexity — Real-time, cited Note synthesis Claude + Notion — Structured output Report generation Opus 4 GPT-4o Deep analysis 🎨 Creative Task Primary Backup Why Image gen Midjourney DALL-E 3 Best quality Text-in-image DALL-E 3 — Only one that renders text 2. Workflow Blueprints📝 Blueprint 1: Technical Blog Post 💻 Blueprint 2: Ship a Feature 📧 Blueprint 3: Email Triage 3. Integration Architectures🅰️ Claude Code as Hub For: Terminal power users, backend devs 🅱️ Cursor as Hub For: Frontend/full-stack, VS Code loyalists 🅲️ Custom Orchestration For: Automation seekers, maximum control 4. Cost Optimization💰 Tiered Model Strategy 📊 Per-Task Cost Estimate Task Model Est. Cost Casual chat Gemini Flash ~$0.01 Code completion GPT-4o mini ~$0.05 Article writing Sonnet 4 ~$0.30 Complex reasoning Opus 4 ~$1.50 🔄 Caching Tactics ✅ Prompt Caching (Claude) — avoid re-sending repeated context ✅ Local conversation cache — reduce context window usage ✅ Batch similar requests — fewer API calls 5. Getting Started 🚀 Don't try to build the perfect workflow on day one. Progressive Roadmap Week Goal Action 1 Get comfortable Pick 1-2 core tools, learn the basics 2 Apply IRL Use in real work, identify pain points 3 Compose Add more tools, start combining Ongoing Optimize Iterate based on feedback Bottom line: Tools are means, productivity is the end. The best workflow is the one that fits you. Start simple, iterate fast. Related: AI Coding Tools Showdown → AI Agents Rising →

title: ABOUT
content: Hey, I'm Chao 👋 console.log("Hello, World! I build things with AI.") I'm an AI Engineer & Full-Stack Developer who's obsessed with pushing the boundaries of what's possible with artificial intelligence. This blog is my digital lab — where I document experiments, share insights, and explore the bleeding edge of tech. 🧠 What I Do 📡 What You'll Find Here Category Content AI Tools Deep dives into Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and emerging tools LLM Analysis Benchmarks, comparisons, and real-world testing of GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama AI Engineering RAG, Fine-tuning, Agent frameworks, Production ML Tech Trends What's next in AI, dev tools, and the future of software 🛠️ Tech Arsenal 📫 Connect "The best way to predict the future is to invent it." — Alan Kay Tech Stack of This Blog: Hexo + Cyberpunk2077 Theme + Giscus · Hosted on GitHub Pages

title: ARCHIVE
content: 📂 ArchiveAll posts, organized by category. Categories Recent PostsBrowse all posts below, or filter by tags on the sidebar. Stay curious. Keep building.

title: FRIENDS
content: 🔗 Link ExchangeQuality tech blogs and resources I recommend. Want to exchange links? Open an issue on GitHub. 🤖 AI & ML Curated list of AI-focused blogs and resources. 💻 Dev & Engineering Engineering blogs worth following. 📬 Add Your LinkSubmit a PR or issue with this format: Requirements: Active tech blog (AI / dev / engineering) Original content HTTPS enabled Let's build the future together.

(function () { "use strict"; var ideas = [ "Ship the smallest useful experiment.", "Turn one repeated task into a prompt.", "Write the boring notes. They become leverage.", "A good workflow removes decisions, not curiosity.", "Measure once before you optimize twice." ]; function ready(fn) { if (document.readyState === "loading") { document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", fn); } else { fn(); } } function ensureProgress() { if (document.getElementById("nf-progress")) return; var bar = document.createElement("div"); bar.id = "nf-progress"; document.body.appendChild(bar); var update = function () { var scrollable = document.documentElement.scrollHeight - window.innerHeight; var ratio = scrollable > 0 ? window.scrollY / scrollable : 0; bar.style.width = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, ratio * 100)) + "%"; }; update(); window.addEventListener("scroll", update, { passive: true }); window.addEventListener("resize", update); } function ensureHeroChip() { var cover = document.querySelector(".kratos-cover-2 .container, .kratos-cover-2"); if (!cover || cover.querySelector(".nf-hero-chip")) return; var chip = document.createElement("div"); chip.className = "nf-hero-chip"; chip.textContent = "AI workflow lab online"; cover.appendChild(chip); } function ensureSignalWidget() { var sidebar = document.querySelector("#kratos-widget-area .sticky-area, #kratos-widget-area"); if (!sidebar || sidebar.querySelector(".nf-signal-widget")) return; var widget = document.createElement("aside"); widget.className = "widget nf-signal-widget clearfix"; widget.innerHTML = [ 'Signal Board', '', signalItem("Agents", 86), signalItem("RAG", 78), signalItem("Coding", 92), "" ].join(""); sidebar.insertBefore(widget, sidebar.firstChild); } function signalItem(label, value) { return [ '', "" + label + "", '', "" + value + "%", "" ].join(""); } function ensureToast() { var toast = document.querySelector(".nf-toast"); if (toast) return toast; toast = document.createElement("div"); toast.className = "nf-toast"; toast.setAttribute("role", "status"); toast.setAttribute("aria-live", "polite"); document.body.appendChild(toast); return toast; } function bindInspiration() { document.addEventListener("click", function (event) { var trigger = event.target.closest("[data-nf-inspire]"); if (!trigger) return; event.preventDefault(); var idea = ideas[Math.floor(Math.random() * ideas.length)]; var toast = ensureToast(); toast.innerHTML = "Next spark" + idea + ""; toast.classList.add("is-visible"); pop(event.clientX, event.clientY); window.clearTimeout(bindInspiration.timer); bindInspiration.timer = window.setTimeout(function () { toast.classList.remove("is-visible"); }, 3200); }); } function pop(x, y) { if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches) return; for (var i = 0; i < 10; i += 1) { var dot = document.createElement("span"); var angle = (Math.PI * 2 * i) / 10; var distance = 42 + Math.random() * 22; dot.className = "nf-pop"; dot.style.left = x + "px"; dot.style.top = y + "px"; dot.style.setProperty("--x", Math.cos(angle) * distance + "px"); dot.style.setProperty("--y", Math.sin(angle) * distance + "px"); document.body.appendChild(dot); dot.addEventListener("animationend", function () { this.remove(); }); } } function ensureStarfield() { if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches) return; if (document.getElementById("nf-starfield")) return; var canvas = document.createElement("canvas"); canvas.id = "nf-starfield"; document.body.prepend(canvas); var ctx = canvas.getContext("2d"); var nodes = []; var width = 0; var height = 0; function resize() { width = canvas.width = window.innerWidth; height = canvas.height = window.innerHeight; var count = Math.min(56, Math.max(28, Math.floor(width / 28))); nodes = Array.from({ length: count }, function () { return { x: Math.random() * width, y: Math.random() * height, vx: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.25, vy: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.25 }; }); } function draw() { ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height); ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(23, 184, 166, 0.42)"; ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(108, 124, 255, 0.12)"; nodes.forEach(function (node, index) { node.x += node.vx; node.y += node.vy; if (node.x < 0 || node.x > width) node.vx *= -1; if (node.y < 0 || node.y > height) node.vy *= -1; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(node.x, node.y, 1.4, 0, Math.PI * 2); ctx.fill(); for (var j = index + 1; j < nodes.length; j += 1) { var other = nodes[j]; var dx = node.x - other.x; var dy = node.y - other.y; var distance = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy); if (distance < 118) { ctx.globalAlpha = 1 - distance / 118; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.moveTo(node.x, node.y); ctx.lineTo(other.x, other.y); ctx.stroke(); ctx.globalAlpha = 1; } } }); window.requestAnimationFrame(draw); } resize(); draw(); window.addEventListener("resize", resize); } ready(function () { ensureProgress(); ensureHeroChip(); ensureSignalWidget(); ensureStarfield(); bindInspiration(); }); document.addEventListener("pjax:complete", function () { ensureHeroChip(); ensureSignalWidget(); }); })();

:root { --nf-accent: #17b8a6; --nf-accent-2: #6c7cff; --nf-warm: #ffbd59; --nf-ink: rgba(18, 24, 38, 0.82); --nf-glass: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78); --nf-line: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.34); } html[data-theme="dark"] { --nf-ink: rgba(238, 244, 255, 0.88); --nf-glass: rgba(18, 24, 38, 0.66); --nf-line: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.14); } #nf-progress { position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; z-index: 9999; width: 0; height: 3px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--nf-accent), var(--nf-accent-2), var(--nf-warm)); box-shadow: 0 0 14px rgba(23, 184, 166, 0.45); pointer-events: none; } #nf-starfield { position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: -1; opacity: 0.38; pointer-events: none; } .kratos-cover-2::after { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: radial-gradient(circle at 18% 24%, rgba(23, 184, 166, 0.2), transparent 24%), radial-gradient(circle at 80% 30%, rgba(108, 124, 255, 0.18), transparent 22%), linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(3, 7, 18, 0.06), rgba(3, 7, 18, 0.18)); pointer-events: none; } .kratos-cover-2 .desc, .kratos-cover-2 .cover-title, .kratos-cover-2 h2, .kratos-cover-2 p { position: relative; z-index: 1; } .nf-hero-chip { position: relative; z-index: 1; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; max-width: min(92vw, 620px); margin-top: 14px; padding: 8px 14px; border: 1px solid var(--nf-line); border-radius: 999px; color: #fff; background: rgba(5, 12, 26, 0.34); box-shadow: 0 12px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.18); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.45; } .nf-hero-chip::before { content: ""; width: 8px; height: 8px; flex: 0 0 auto; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--nf-accent); box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px rgba(23, 184, 166, 0.16), 0 0 18px rgba(23, 184, 166, 0.8); } .kratos-hentry, .widget { transition: transform 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease; } .kratos-hentry:hover, .widget:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 20px 44px rgba(20, 28, 45, 0.14); } html[data-theme="dark"] .kratos-hentry:hover, html[data-theme="dark"] .widget:hover { box-shadow: 0 20px 44px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.28); } .kratos-entry-thumb-img { transition: transform 380ms ease, filter 380ms ease; } .kratos-entry-thumb:hover .kratos-entry-thumb-img { transform: scale(1.035); filter: saturate(1.12) contrast(1.04); } .nf-signal-widget { overflow: hidden; } .nf-signal-title { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 10px; color: var(--nf-ink); font-weight: 700; } .nf-signal-dot { width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--nf-accent); box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px rgba(23, 184, 166, 0.14); } .nf-signal-track { display: grid; gap: 8px; } .nf-signal-item { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 72px minmax(0, 1fr) 38px; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-size: 12px; color: var(--nf-ink); } .nf-signal-bar { position: relative; height: 7px; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(127, 143, 166, 0.18); } .nf-signal-bar span { position: absolute; inset: 0 auto 0 0; width: var(--value); border-radius: inherit; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--nf-accent), var(--nf-accent-2)); } .nf-toast { position: fixed; right: 22px; bottom: 92px; z-index: 9998; max-width: min(320px, calc(100vw - 32px)); padding: 14px 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.28); border-radius: 14px; color: #fff; background: rgba(14, 22, 38, 0.86); box-shadow: 0 20px 54px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.28); backdrop-filter: blur(14px); opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 180ms ease, transform 180ms ease; } .nf-toast.is-visible { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } .nf-toast strong { display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; color: #9cf1e8; } .kr-tool .tool .nf-inspire-box a { display: grid; width: 30px; height: 30px; place-items: center; } .kr-tool .tool .nf-inspire-box span { color: var(--nf-accent); } .nf-pop { position: fixed; z-index: 9997; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--nf-accent); pointer-events: none; animation: nf-pop 700ms ease forwards; } @keyframes nf-pop { to { transform: translate(var(--x), var(--y)) scale(0); opacity: 0; } } @media (max-width: 767px) { .nf-hero-chip { padding: 7px 12px; font-size: 12px; } .nf-toast { right: 16px; bottom: 82px; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { #nf-starfield, .nf-pop { display: none; } *, *::before, *::after { transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; } }
